# Momentum relationship with mass and velocity

## Short Teaching Summary
Momentum relationship with mass and velocity: A doubling of the mass results in a doubling of the momentum when velocity is constant. Key detail: A quadrupling in velocity results in a quadrupling of the momentum when mass is constant. Key detail: A doubling of the mass results in a doubling of the momentum (if velocity stays the same). Key detail: A quadrupling in velocity results in a quadrupling of the momentum (if mass stays the same).

## Best Examples
- A doubling of the mass results in a doubling of the momentum when velocity is constant.
- A quadrupling in velocity results in a quadrupling of the momentum when mass is constant.
- A doubling of the mass results in a doubling of the momentum (if velocity stays the same).
